frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
generated_comment: Yaar 8 saal mein sirf 2 saal kaam kiya aur ab 50% maang rahi hai? This is exactly why I say shaadi aajkal ek business deal ban gayi hai for women — invest nothing, walk away with everything. Seedha ulta dowry hai bhai, bas groom ki taraf se. No wonder smart men are avoiding marriage altogether. Prenup mandatory hona chahiye India mein, tabhi ye gold-digger culture band hogi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment generalizes from one case to frame marriage as a financial trap set by women, using the 'gold-digger' stereotype and implying women marry primarily for financial extraction rather than genuine partnership. It encodes institutional misogyny by framing legal alimony rights as predatory female behavior while presenting itself as a rational legal-reform argument.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 36. Single. A divorced man living in Delhi. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post about a celebrity couple's divorce where the wife is seeking 50 percent of her husband's assets despite having worked for only two years of their eight year marriage. You are commenting on whether what she is asking for is justified and what it says about why women get married.
